question: What does an interface describe in TypeScript?
options: A function's return type only
The shape of an object — which fields it has and what types they are
A class hierarchy
A module's exports

question: How do you make a field optional in an interface?
options: Add ! after the field name
Add optional: true
Add ? after the field name
Use the Optional<T> wrapper

question: What does readonly do on an interface field?
options: Makes the field invisible outside the module
Prevents the field from being assigned after the object is created
Makes the field optional
Allows any type to be assigned

question: You define interface Dog { name: string } and interface Cat { name: string }. Can a function typed as (animal: Dog) accept a Cat object?
options: No — the types have different names
Only if you use a type cast
Yes — TypeScript uses structural typing, so matching shape is enough
Only if Cat extends Dog

question: For typing plain objects, which should you prefer — interface or type alias?
options: type alias — it is always more powerful
interface — it gives better error messages and supports extends
They are completely identical, it does not matter
interface only works with classes
